category_title: Students with internalizing behaviors and low self-confidence
minimal_context: The student is in an afternoon nurture group session and is asked to transition to key skills academic work.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to transition to our afternoon key skills work.

STUDENT: (Sits apart from the other pupils, experiencing social isolation, and does not prepare for the activity.)

TUTOR: Please get your materials ready for the task.

STUDENT: (Displays low curricular engagement and avoids looking at the assignment.)

TUTOR: We need to start the academic work now.

STUDENT: Oh, no, do we have to do key skills? Oh, no, why do I got to do work?

options.A: Conduct functional behavior analyses targeting purported variables and subject them to data-based hypothesis testing.
options.B: Explicitly incorporate and promote self-compassion within the key skills intervention content during afternoon sessions.
options.C: Implement a highly routinized day with set structures for academic work, social time, play, and dining.
options.D: Review a behavior contract with the student, outline both the behavior expectations and corresponding rewards, and require them to sign it.
